name: Corn Sticks (Surullitos)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the water and salt in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
2. Slowly pour in the cornmeal while stirring continuously until the mixture is thick and smooth.
3. Remove from heat and stir in the cheese. Mix well and let cool.
4. Heat about 1/3 cup vegetable oil in a large, heavy skillet.
5. Take small portions of the dough and shape into sticks that are approximately 3 inches long and 1 inch in diameter.
6. Carefully drop the shaped sticks into the hot oil and fry until golden brown on all sides.
7. Using tongs or a slotted spoon, remove the corn sticks from the oil and drain on paper towel to prevent them from becoming soggy.
Yield: 18 Corn Sticks
Note: These Puerto Rican snacks are traditionally served warm as a side dish alongside a drink and can be enjoyed with meat or fish.
